Actual recorded duration; target is 10000 ms
Grip cycles per second - primary metric
Coefficient of variation of inter-grip intervals (%). Lower = more regular. Note: the app displays this inverted but stores raw CV.

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: none, No weakness | mild, Mild | moderate, Moderate | severe, Severe
Field Note: Self-reported degree of weakness in the tested hand

Array of touch events serialised as JSON. Each event: {t, x, y, type, touch_id, rejected, reject_reason}

e.g. iOS 17.4 or Android 14
Semantic version string (e.g. 1.2.0)
UUID v4 - used for deduplication on re-sync
Stride-adjusted distance at which the audio turnaround beep fired. Computed at trial start as max(1.5, 3.0 - 0.8 * gait_speed) using calibrated stride and step time. Varies per participant.

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: stillness, Sustained stillness (normal) | sitdown_timeout, Sit-down 30 s watchdog | manual, Emergency Stop pressed | safety_timeout, 2 min safety cap
Field Note: How the trial ended - QC marker
Primary clinical metric. Elapsed time from trial start (go cue) to sit-down stillness detection, in seconds. Thresholds: <10s normal, 10-20s variable, >=13.5s fall-risk.
Soft proxy for stand-up duration: time from go cue to first detected step.
Step count during the walk-out segment (first step to cue beep).
Estimated distance during walk-out segment. Beep fires at the stride-adjusted target (see tug_cue_distance_m), not always at exactly 3.0 m.
Duration of walk-out segment (first detected step to cue beep).
Mean stride length over walk-out segment (Weinberg estimator). First stride excluded.
Coefficient of variation of stride length (SD / mean). Higher = more irregular gait.
Steps per minute. Mathematically equivalent to 60000 / tug_walk_out_avg_step_time - reported for clinical convenience (spm is the standard unit in gait literature).
Mean inter-step interval over walk-out segment. Reported in ms alongside its CV.
Coefficient of variation of inter-step interval. Validated fall-risk predictor.
Walk-out distance / duration. Clinical cutoff <0.8 m/s suggests mobility impairment.
ISO 8601 UTC. Snapshotted from UserProfile.tug_step_calibration so each TUG row knows which calibration produced its template.
Total accepted W-windows averaged into the mean template during calibration. Sole calibration-quality field on each row.

Runtime detection threshold. Sliding-window template correlation must exceed this value to count as a step. Equals 0.85 * median(per-window calibration correlations) - functions as the noise floor in correlation space.
Mean Weinberg stride length learned during calibration. Used by the cue-distance formula.
Mean inter-step interval observed during calibration. Used by the cue-distance formula.

JSON array of ~36 floats: the normalised mean W-shape template (zero-mean, unit-norm). Combine with tug_cal_template_dt_ms + tug_cal_correlation_floor to re-run step detection on tug_raw_json in post-processing.
Time between successive samples of the template. Currently 17 ms (~60 Hz) - kept as a field so analysts don't have to hard-code it.
App version when the calibration was created. Distinct from tug_app_version (the version that ran this test).

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: none, None | cane, Cane | walker, Walker

Field Note: Free-text reason provided by participant or clinician

JSON array of two interleaved event kinds. Motion (~60 Hz): {kind: 'motion', t (ms since start), v (m/s2 - gravity-projected vertical user acceleration; precomputed at capture time as dot(rawAccel - gravity, normalize(gravity)) where gravity is low-passed raw accel at alpha=0.1)}. Timer (exactly 2 per trial): {kind: 'timer', t (ms; start at 0), event: 'start' or 'stop', source: 'sensor' (auto stillness-stop) or 'manual'}. Raw 3-axis accel and gyro are NOT stored - only the vertical channel used by the step detector and Weinberg stride estimator.
SHA-256 hex digest of raw_data array
